<!-- source-hash: 9eed8dfc26c3d6b242e099ffb8c7bffc -->
DTO representing a complete device record returned by the OpenFrame API, encapsulating hardware identity, network details, OS metadata, lifecycle timestamps, and associated tags.

## Key Components

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `String` | Unique device identifier |
| `machineId` | `String` | Machine-level identifier |
| `hostname` / `displayName` | `String` | Network hostname and human-readable name |
| `ip` / `macAddress` | `String` | Network addressing fields |
| `status` | `DeviceStatus` | Current device status enum |
| `type` | `DeviceType` | Device classification enum |
| `osType` / `osVersion` / `osBuild` | `String` | Operating system metadata |
| `agentVersion` | `String` | Installed agent version |
| `lastSeen` / `registeredAt` / `updatedAt` | `Instant` | Lifecycle timestamps |
| `tags` | `List<DeviceTagResponse>` | Key-value tags assigned to the device |

Lombok annotations (`@Data`, `@Builder`, `@NoArgsConstructor`, `@AllArgsConstructor`) handle boilerplate. `@Schema` annotations drive OpenAPI/Swagger documentation.

## Usage Example

```java
// Building a DeviceResponse manually (e.g., in a mapper or test)
DeviceResponse response = DeviceResponse.builder()
    .id("device-123")
    .machineId("machine-123")
    .hostname("server-01")
    .displayName("Production Server 01")
    .ip("192.168.1.100")
    .macAddress("00:11:22:33:44:55")
    .agentVersion("1.2.3")
    .status(DeviceStatus.ONLINE)
    .type(DeviceType.SERVER)
    .osType("Linux")
    .osVersion("Ubuntu 20.04")
    .manufacturer("Dell")
    .model("PowerEdge R740")
    .organizationId("org-456")
    .registeredAt(Instant.now())
    .lastSeen(Instant.now())
    .tags(List.of(new DeviceTagResponse("env", "production")))
    .build();
```
